title: Understanding Weight on the Moon
text: The weight of an object is the force exerted on it due to gravity. On the Moon, gravity is one-sixth of that on Earth.

title: Weight Formula
text: The weight of an object can be calculated using the formula: weight $$=$$ mass * gravitational acceleration.

title: Gravitational Acceleration on the Moon
text: On the Moon, the gravitational acceleration is one-sixth of Earth's gravitational acceleration, which is approximately $$9.8$$ m/s².

title: Calculate Mass
text: Given that the weight on the Moon is $$250$$ N and the gravitational acceleration on the Moon is $$\frac{9.8}{6}$$ m/s², use the formula weight $$=$$ mass * gravitational acceleration to find the mass.
